story-task.title: Verify ExtensionContext exposes currentModel
story-task.description:
Inspect pi-agent-core's ExtensionContext type to confirm whether currentModel (or equivalent) is available to extensions at dispatch time. If not, the task widens into a prerequisite against pi-agent-core — escalate to user for scope decision.

## TASK-001-01 completion finding (2026-05-03)

**ExtensionContext.model** is confirmed at `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts:219`:

```typescript
model: Model<any> | undefined;
```

`Model<TApi>` at `node_modules/@mariozechner/pi-ai/dist/types.d.ts:378` exposes:

```typescript
id: string;       // e.g. "anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6"
provider: Provider;  // Provider = KnownProvider | string — "openrouter", "anthropic", etc.
api: TApi;        // "anthropic-messages" | "openai-completions" | ...
```

**Resolution**: DEC-0001's chosen option is implementable today. Bare `model:` ids (no `/`) can resolve `provider` from `ctx.model.provider` and `modelId` from the spec string. Fully-qualified specs (`openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`) are unaffected — `parseModelSpec` splits on `/` as before.

**Caveat**: `ctx.model` is typed `Model<any> | undefined`. The `| undefined` means the dispatch path must handle the case where no session model is active. Whether `ctx.model` is populated at classify time (monitor `agent_end` / `tool_call` events) is not type-enforceable — it's an empirical question. The type contract says it *should* be populated during any agent turn, but the code path that sets it is internal to pi-core. The dispatch must produce a clear error ("Cannot resolve bare model id 'X': no session model context available"), not a silent fallback to `anthropic`.
story-task.acceptance: Written confirmation in the task record of what ExtensionContext currently exposes and whether DEC-0001's chosen option is implementable today or depends on upstream changes

story-task.title: Implement bare-id resolution in pi-jit-agents
story-task.description: Add model-resolution logic at executeAgent. Bare ids trigger ExtensionContext lookup; fully-qualified specs pin.
story-task.acceptance: executeAgent resolves bare ids via ExtensionContext, surfaces clear error on missing binding, preserves fully-qualified semantics

story-task.title: Unit tests for model resolution
story-task.acceptance: Three-way coverage (bare + session, bare + no session, fully-qualified) all passing

story-task.title: Add pre-dispatch check in executeAgent
story-task.acceptance: Pre-dispatch branch inspects toolChoice and compiled spec's thinking field; when incompatible, disables thinking and emits warning

story-task.title: Define structured diagnostic channel
story-task.description: Decide whether warnings emit via logger, alongside JitAgentResult, or both. Must be consumable by both pi-workflows (workflow steps) and pi-behavior-monitors (classify calls).
story-task.acceptance: JitAgentResult gains a diagnostics field or equivalent; both consumers can read it
